channel.title: BimaNiti — Insurance & Market Insights
channel.description: Analysing markets, decoding insurance, and sharing insights that matter.

item.title: Insurance Consumer Court Rulings Roundup: Key Decisions Every Policyholder Should Know
item.description: From the Supreme Court's landmark 'fraud vitiates all' ruling to the NCDRC ordering HDFC Life and LIC to pay claims with compensation — three recent consumer court decisions that clarify policyholder rights in India.

item.title: IRDAI's 2026 Health Insurance Overhaul: 8 Changes That Reshaped Coverage for Every Indian
item.description: From removing the age bar for buying health insurance to slashing the moratorium period from 8 to 5 years, IRDAI's 2026 regulatory overhaul has fundamentally changed health insurance in India. Here's what every policyholder needs to know.

item.title: Insurtech Funding Winter: What H1 2026's 83% Collapse Means for Indian Insurance
item.description: Indian insurtech startups raised just $45M in H1 2026, down 83% from H1 2025. Is this a temporary funding winter or a structural reset for the insurtech ecosystem? We analyse the data, the survivors, and what comes next.

item.title: LIC Ordered to Pay Rs 60 Lakh+ to Policyholder for Claim Delay and Deficiency
item.description: The NCDRC ordered LIC to pay over Rs 60 lakh including compensation for delayed claim settlement and deficiency in service, setting a precedent for timely claim processing.

item.title: Supreme Court: Fraud Vitiates All - Landmark Ruling on Insurance Contracts
item.description: The Supreme Court held that fraud vitiates all insurance contracts, ruling that insurers can repudiate claims if the policyholder committed fraud, even if the claim itself is genuine. The landmark judgment settles a decades-old legal debate.

item.title: PMFBY 2026: How Satellite Tech and KCC Linkage Are Fixing India's Crop Insurance Problem
item.description: The revamped PMFBY for 2026 introduces satellite-based yield assessment (replacing yield tests), automatic KCC linkage, and voluntary participation. Will these changes finally deliver on the promise of crop insurance for Indian farmers?

item.title: Bima Sugam Countdown: What to Expect as Full Launch Nears
item.description: Bima Sugam's full transactional launch is expected by end-September 2026. The zero-commission digital marketplace could reshape insurance distribution in India. Here's what policyholders and insurers should watch for.

item.title: GIFT City Emerges as Insurance Hub with 20+ Insurers, 100% FDI Route for Reinsurers
item.description: More than 20 insurers and reinsurers have set up operations at GIFT City's IFSC, as the government positions the hub as a global reinsurance destination with 100% FDI and tax incentives.

item.title: Supreme Court Directs IRDAI to Clarify Cross-Border Motor TP Insurance Clauses
item.description: The Supreme Court directed IRDAI to issue clear guidelines on cross-border application of Motor Third Party insurance clauses, bringing clarity to claims involving vehicles traveling between India and Nepal, Bangladesh, and Bhutan.

item.title: IRDAI Seeks Comments on Rs 800 Crore Policyholders' Education and Protection Fund Regulations
item.description: IRDAI issued a consultation paper on the proposed regulatory framework for the Rs 800 crore Policyholders' Education and Protection Fund, seeking stakeholder comments by July 13, 2026.

item.title: GIFT City vs Mainland Insurance: The New Regulatory Arbitrage and What It Means
item.description: With 20+ insurers now in GIFT City and a 100% FDI route bypassing the mainland's 74% cap, a two-track insurance system is emerging in India. We explore the regulatory arbitrage, the winners, and the risks.

item.title: ICICI Lombard Q1 FY27: Fire Losses, Motor Reserves, and the Profit Squeeze
item.description: ICICI Lombard's Q1 profit slumped 46% as large fire claims and the Supreme Court's Motor TP verdict hit earnings. We analyse whether this is a one-off or a warning sign for India's largest private general insurer.

item.title: HDFC Life Ordered to Pay Rs 50 Lakh to Nominee Despite Disclosure Lapse
item.description: The NCDRC ordered HDFC Life to pay Rs 50 lakh to a policy nominee, ruling that minor disclosure lapses do not justify claim repudiation when the policy has been in force for several years.

item.title: GST ITC Impact on Insurers: 10 Months On
item.description: Ten months after the GST exemption on health insurance, the input tax credit (ITC) loss is unevenly distributed — Niva Bupa absorbed ~4%, Star Health ~1%, and Care Health ~2%. Net customer benefit stands at 12-15%.

item.title: Life Insurance Sector Begins FY27 with 16.6% NBP Growth
item.description: India's life insurance sector began FY27 with robust new business premiums growing 16.6%, with private insurers significantly outpacing the market and increasing their share to nearly 40%.

item.title: ICICI Lombard Q1 FY27: Profit Slumps 46% on Large Fire Claims, Motor TP Verdict
item.description: ICICI Lombard reported a 46% YoY decline in Q1 FY27 net profit to Rs 403 crore, hit by large fire insurance claims and increased motor TP reserves following a Supreme Court verdict.

item.title: PMFBY 2026 Revamp: Satellite Tech, KCC Linkage, and Voluntary Opt-In for All Farmers
item.description: The government has unveiled a major revamp of PMFBY for 2026, introducing satellite-based yield assessment (SATYBT), automatic KCC-linked coverage, and making the scheme voluntary for all farmers.

item.title: HDFC Life & ICICI Pru Q1 FY27: Double-Digit Profit Growth
item.description: HDFC Life reported Q1 FY27 PAT of Rs 611 crore (+12%), while ICICI Pru posted PAT of Rs 386 crore (+27.8%), with retail protection APE surging 60.4%.

item.title: IRDAI Launches Health Insurance Scorecards for 15 Insurers to Boost Transparency
item.description: IRDAI launched health insurance scorecards for 15 major insurers, publicly ranking them on claim settlement ratio, grievance redressal speed, cashless approval rate, and policy retention metrics.

item.title: IRDAI Proposes Overhaul of Insurance Commission Structure, Caps on High-Reward Products
item.description: IRDAI has proposed a comprehensive overhaul of the insurance commission structure, including caps on commissions for high-premium products and a renewed focus on need-based selling over commission-driven distribution.
